name: secrets-guard
description: Keep secrets out of the model: block plaintext secrets in prompts, resolve vault references (Keeper/1Password) into tool input, and redact secrets leaked in tool output.

vault_provider.title: Vault provider
vault_provider.description: Which secret vault to resolve references from: auto (Keeper, then 1Password), keeper, or 1password.

op_account.title: 1Password account
op_account.description: Account (shorthand, sign-in address, or ID) to pass to `op` on machines with multiple 1Password accounts. Leave empty if you only have one.

block_on_prompt_secret.title: Block prompts with plaintext secrets
block_on_prompt_secret.description: When true, a prompt containing a plaintext secret is blocked before it reaches the model.

tool_input_policy.title: Tool input policy
tool_input_policy.description: Action when a tool call carries a plaintext secret: deny, redact, or warn.

tool_output_mode.title: Tool output mode
tool_output_mode.description: Action on secrets leaked in tool output: redact, block, or off.

command_references.title: Command references
command_references.description: Whether to replace vault references with their value in Bash command bodies: 'inject' (default) injects the value, 'keep' leaves every reference literal. Either way, references are always resolved internally so the output is redacted; commands that resolve references themselves (op read, ksm notation, secrets-guard read/run) always keep them literal, and a single occurrence can opt out with a leading backslash (\op://...).

custom_patterns_path.title: Custom patterns file
custom_patterns_path.description: JSON file of additional {category, pattern} detection rules.

allowlist_path.title: Allowlist file
allowlist_path.description: File of regexes (one per line) whose matches are treated as non-secrets.

audit_log_path.title: Audit log file
audit_log_path.description: Path to append value-free audit records (JSON lines).

execution_mode.title: Execution mode
execution_mode.description: Where secret resolution happens: 'auto' (detect — Cowork host uses the sealed-box disk channel, plain Claude Code resolves locally), 'local' (always resolve on this host), or 'cowork' (force the Cowork host daemon + sealed-box disk channel). Cowork is detected automatically via CLAUDE_CODE_IS_COWORK.

cowork_spool.title: Cowork spool path
cowork_spool.description: Host path of the shared Cowork 'outputs' mount. Auto-derived from CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR; set only to override. The host writes the sealed control-plane here (public keys + ciphertext, never a secret value).

cowork_isolate.title: Cowork VM isolation
cowork_isolate.description: When true, also add a pid/mount-proc namespace to the per-command sandbox so other VM processes cannot inspect its /proc. Defense-in-depth on top of the never-transmitted private key; off by default because it changes the child's process view.

cowork_ref_policy.title: Cowork reference policy
cowork_ref_policy.description: 'audit' (default) resolves any reference the per-command one-time-token-authenticated request asks for and logs each; 'enforce' resolves only references the host observed being written this session (stricter, but breaks transparent rendering of references the host never saw).

sandbox.title: Secret-rendering sandbox
sandbox.description: Render vault references into real values — in the environment, in files under the working directory, AND in the command body — scoped to one command, so apps that read secrets from config files just work. OFF by default: the product's primary guarantee is redaction (no vault value reaches the model), not reference rendering, so apps just read their own local files (e.g. a .env holding the real value) and the model only ever sees the redacted form. 'on' enables rendering (Linux uses a private bind-mount; macOS/Windows render in place and restore via a crash-recovery journal); 'auto' enables it wherever a vault resolves; 'off' (default) disables it. The redaction guard runs regardless. In Cowork the sandbox is the value-delivery channel and is always on.

preload_secrets.title: Proactive full-vault redaction guard
preload_secrets.description: Load every value the vault exposes into memory and redact or block any of them (in any encoding) in prompts, tool inputs, tool outputs and file reads — even values never referenced this session — so a known vault secret can never reach the model. On macOS/Linux the values live in the per-session cache; on Windows in the sandbox-dlp service (the credential holder). 'auto' (default) and 'on' enable it; 'off' limits the guard to values resolved during the session.

guard_required.title: Fail-closed policy
guard_required.description: What to do when the redaction guard cannot verify a text (on Windows: the sandbox-dlp service is unreachable). 'auto' (default) fails closed only where the service is actually running, so a machine that never installed it degrades to the built-in pattern detector instead of blocking every prompt and tool (an incomplete install does not brick the CLI). 'on' always fails closed when the guard is unavailable (strict; for fleets that guarantee the service). 'off' never fails closed.

sandbox_globs.title: Sandbox file globs
sandbox_globs.description: Comma-separated filename globs the sandbox scans for references under the working directory, overriding the default set (.env, .env.*, *.env, *.yaml, *.yml, *.json, *.toml, *.ini, *.properties, *.conf, *.cfg, *.config, *.tfvars, *.envrc). Keep it targeted so the scan stays fast on large repos.

shell_tools.title: Extra shell tool names
shell_tools.description: Comma-separated extra tool names to treat as shell command execution (wrap + redact), beyond the built-in 'Bash' and the MCP shell pattern (any name ending in '__bash'/'__shell', e.g. Cowork's mcp__workspace__bash). Use only if a host exposes a shell under a name the pattern doesn't catch.

kernel_dlp.title: Kernel-enforced file rendering (deprecated, no-op)
kernel_dlp.description: DEPRECATED / no-op. secrets-guard no longer uses WinFsp or a system service: it runs entirely per-user (the local ksm/op profile + an in-memory cache) and needs no administrator rights. This option is kept only for backward compatibility and is ignored. The sandbox, when enabled, uses the in-place renderer with a crash-recovery journal.

dlp_install_source.title: sandbox-dlp installer source
dlp_install_source.description: Override the base URL the plugin downloads the sandbox-dlp installer from (for air-gapped or on-prem mirrors). Leave empty to use the default GitHub release.
